channel.title: Resource | FORRT - Framework for Open and Reproducible Research Training
channel.copyright: © 2026 - FORRT > Framework for Open and Reproducible Research Training

item.title: Chinese-language Glossary of Open Scholarship terms
item.description:
FORRT’s Glossary of Open Scholarship terms is now available in Chinese, lowering the barrier to understanding and participating in open-science discussions for Chinese-speaking researchers and students. It joins the existing English, German, and Arabic versions.

Explore the glossary

item.title: Nowhere Lab: an online community for those without a lab
item.description:
FORRT welcomes Nowhere Lab into our community - a space for anyone without a lab who wants the lab-meeting experience — new faculty, ex-academics, freelancers, and keen undergrads. Their members have also produced a PhD Survival Guide. Check the website, or join their channel on Slack.

Read more

item.title: JUST-OS chatbot launched to help navigate Open Science resources
item.description:
JUST-OS, an AI-based chatbot developed by researchers at the University of Groningen and FORRT, is now live on our website. It gives user-friendly, tailored guidance to help researchers find relevant Open Science resources. Try it out and share your feedback.

Try the chatbot

item.title: Updated Get Involved page
item.description:
The Get Involved page has been updated and rebuilt, and we have made it easier to keep the updates coming. So if you are looking for ways to get involved, have a look at the current opportunities now.

Get involved
